violations.text: The bathroom hand sinks in the first-floor restrooms and the thrid-floor men's restroom are lacking signage notifying employees when and where to wash their hands prior to returning to work. New Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2025

Soap and paper towels were not available at the hand-washing sink in the second-floor bar area. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap, and paper towels. Paper towels and soap were provided during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC notified. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Raw animal foods (such as shell eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as prepared sauces and ice cream mix) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food items were reorganized to follow proper storage instructions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Cooked mussels were observed thawed in their original intact reduced oxygen packaging (ROP). Seafood items packaged in ROP shall be removed from their original packaging prior to thawing. Food items were observed within temperature range of handling instructions on packaging and were removed from packaging during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Ice cream mix were observed stored on the floor in the first-floor walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Food items were moved onto shelving within unit during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

The prep unit cutting boards in the first-floor kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

A hose is connected to the faucet next to the hand sink in the second-floor kitchen. This faucet is lacking a backflow prevention device. An approved backflow prevention device is required in order to prevent backflow of a solid, liquid, or gas contaminant into the water supply at each point of use at a food establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap in the first-floor kitchen. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. An air break was observed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
The floor in the first and second floor kitchens are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2026

The drain for the 3-bay sink in the first-floor bar area was observed to be leaking into a bucket and is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2025
